query: audit
ok im about to push this feature/user-auth branch, can you review my changes before i push? i've been working on it for a few days and want to make sure i didn't leave anything embarrassing in there
pre-push check on this branch please
is this branch ready to merge? go through the diff and let me know if there are any issues i should fix first

we've been seeing intermittent deadlocks in our worker pool and i think there might be race conditions in the threading code. can you do a thorough concurrency audit of src/workers/ and src/queue/? look for shared mutable state, missing locks, that kind of thing

i'm worried our test suite is giving us false confidence — a lot of tests just check that stuff doesn't crash rather than asserting real behavior. can you audit the test quality across the whole project? look at assertion quality, flaky patterns, isolation issues

we're seeing memory growth in production after a few hours. can you do a resource management audit? check for file handles that aren't closed, connections not returned to pool, temp files piling up, that sort of thing

the api docs in our README say we support 12 endpoints but i'm pretty sure we only implemented like 8 of them. can you do a feature completeness audit — compare what the docs claim vs what's actually in the code?
run a performance audit on this codebase — we're seeing p99 latencies spike under load. look for N+1 queries, unbounded caches, unnecessary allocations in hot paths, blocking calls in async code

we have a CLAUDE.md that says 'prefer composition over inheritance' and 'no global mutable state' but i suspect we've drifted from those principles. can you audit the codebase against our own stated design philosophy?

do a security audit of this project — we're about to go through SOC2 and i want to find any SQL injection, XSS, or hardcoded credentials before the auditors do. focus on the API routes in src/api/ and the auth middleware

our frontend has gotten really inconsistent — different pages use different spacing, fonts keep changing, buttons look different everywhere. can you review the UI design using those CRAP design principles? contrast, repetition, alignment, proximity
can you write unit tests for the UserService class in src/services/user_service.py? it needs tests for create_user, update_user, and delete_user methods. use pytest fixtures for the db connection
there's a bug in the payment processing — when someone enters a negative amount it doesn't throw an error, it just processes it. can you fix the validation in src/payments.py?
review this pull request: https://github.com/acme/api/pull/342 — it's from a junior dev and adds a caching layer. let me know if the approach looks solid
refactor the database module to use connection pooling instead of creating a new connection per request. the current implementation is in src/db/connection.py
help me write a good commit message for these changes — i refactored the order processing pipeline and split the monolithic function into smaller composable steps
set up a github actions CI pipeline for this repo. it should run pytest on every push to main and on all PRs, with python 3.11 and postgres for the integration tests
i need to add rate limiting to our API. can you implement a token bucket algorithm in src/middleware/rate_limiter.py that limits to 100 requests per minute per API key?

can you optimize this SQL query? it's doing a full table scan on the orders table and takes 30 seconds on production. here's the query: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status = 'pending' ORDER BY created_at
explain how the authentication middleware works in src/middleware/auth.ts — i need to understand the JWT flow before i modify it
compare the main branch with the staging branch and tell me what's different. i need to know if staging has any hotfixes that haven't been merged back
we need to add dark mode support to our React app. the design tokens are in src/theme.ts and we're using styled-components. can you set up the theme switching infrastructure?
i need to migrate our database from PostgreSQL to MySQL. can you write the migration scripts and update the ORM config? we're using SQLAlchemy
